content: Workforce metrics include: (1) Density - physicians/nurses per 1,000 population (US: 2.6 physicians, 14.5 nurses; UK: 2.8 physicians, 7.9 nurses; Sub-Saharan Africa median: 0.2 physicians, 1.5 nurses); (2) Productivity - patient encounters per provider per day (primary care: 15-25 typical); (3) Retention rates - % remaining in rural practice after 5 years (40-60% without interventions, 70-80% with comprehensive support); (4) Vacancy rates - open positions as % of budgeted FTE (rural hospitals: 10-20% vs urban 5-8%); (5) Burnout measures - Maslach Burnout Inventory scores. Physician aging is a concern: 40% of US physicians >55 years old, with 1/3 of family physicians planning retirement within 5 years. Projected US shortage: 37,800-124,000 physicians by 2034.
